Promise.headline: Woven in Jaipur. Lived with {italic}.
Promise.label: II — Foreword
Promise.body: Each Carpetstory piece is woven by a single artisan, signed by hand, and travels from one room in Jaipur to another, anywhere in the world.

Making.label: III — The making
Making.headline: Eight months of work pass between the first thread and the last. We don't hurry it.
Making.frame1Caption: The Persian knot, tied left over right, repeated a million times.
Making.frame2Alt: Skeins of dyed yarn in shaft of light
Making.frame2Caption: The dyer reads the colour by eye.
Making.frame3Alt: A wide shot of a horizontal loom with warp threads strung taut
Making.frame3Caption: Each loom belongs to one weaver, start to finish. No assembly line.
Making.frame4Alt: Dye vats, steam rising, hands lifting yarn
Making.frame4Caption: The dyer reads the color by eye. Same recipe, slightly different sky, a different red.
Making.frame4Meta: No two batches are identical. We consider this a feature.
Making.frame5Alt: Yarn drying on rooftops in afternoon sun
Making.frame5Caption: The Rajasthani sun fixes the dye in a way no machine has matched.
Making.frame6Alt: A weaver at scale of the loom, distant
Making.frame6Caption: His name is Salim. His father wove rugs. His grandfather wove rugs. The fourth generation is still deciding.
Making.frame6Meta: Every piece carries the weaver's initials in the selvedge.
Making.frame7Alt: Pile being trimmed with shears, fine clippings falling
Making.frame7Alt2: Detail of clippings collecting on the workshop floor
Making.frame7Caption: The pile is sheared by hand to a millimetre's tolerance.
Making.frame8Alt: Finished rug unrolled on a stone floor
Making.frame8Caption: And then, after eight months — the floor.

Knot.label: IV — A study in scale
Knot.hundredSub: One square inch. The weaver works at this density, knot by knot, for eight months.
Knot.tagline: A number that begins to explain the price. And the wait.

Materials.label: V — The materials
Materials.headline: A short and considered list of things that touch the floor.
Materials.woolDesc: Sourced from highland sheep in Bikaner, where the wool is long-fibered, lanolin-rich, and naturally lustrous. Hand-spun, never machine-carded.
Materials.silkDesc: Mulberry silk drawn from Karnataka. Used as accent threads in the field, where it catches light differently across the day. Reserved for the finer weaves.
Materials.cottonDesc: The warp. The architecture beneath the pile. Long-staple cotton, twisted to a density that holds the knots for a hundred years if cared for.
Materials.dyesHeadline: Three plants. A river. A roof.
Materials.dyesBody: Every colour in a Carpetstory piece begins as a plant, ground or boiled or fermented, then dipped, dried, and dipped again until the wool refuses any more.

Collection.label: I — The collection
Collection.headline: Six pieces. Chosen, not catalogued.
Collection.khwabDesc: A deep madder field with a single ivory medallion, drawn from a 1920s archive.
Collection.saanjhDesc: Undyed wool, dusk tones, the colour of light at the end of the day.
Collection.mehfilDesc: Indigo and silk. A geometric meeting of straight lines and curved restraint.
Collection.shubhDesc: The auspicious one. Walnut and madder tones, woven as a wedding piece.
Collection.naqshDesc: An architectural piece. Sharp lines, deep colour, designed for considered rooms.
Collection.aaraamDesc: The quiet one. Cream, ivory, the faintest blush. For rooms that ask for stillness.

Heritage.label: VI — Heritage
Heritage.headline: Four generations of keeping the dust out of the dye.
Heritage.p1Body: It started with a single loom in a courtyard in Jaipur. My great-grandfather wove pieces for the local merchants. He was known for a specific madder red that no one else could replicate.
Heritage.p2Body: My father expanded the workshop. We began exporting to Europe, but the rules remained the same: natural dyes, hand-spun wool, and the Persian knot. We refused to adopt the tufting guns that were speeding up the industry.
Heritage.p3Body: Carpetstory is still a family operation. We don't have a factory. We have a network of master weavers, some of whom have worked with our family for three generations. The madder red is still exactly the same.

Letter.label: From the founder
Letter.p1: My grandfather wove. My father sold. I noticed, in between, that the world had stopped looking at the floor.
Letter.p2: Carpetstory is a small attempt to make people look down again. Not at the rug — at the eight months it took, and the hands that took them.

World.label: VII — In the world
World.headline: Where they live.
World.caption1: Khwab in a Brooklyn townhouse — Quincy Architects
World.caption2: Aaraam in a Hampstead drawing room — Studio Iro
World.caption3: Shubh in a Marylebone apartment — Private client
World.caption4: Naqsh in a Parisian pied-à-terre — Studio Marais

Testimonials.label: VIII — Voices
Testimonials.q1: It arrived in a wooden crate that smelled of cedar. The rug smelled of wool and sun. Eight months later, both still do.
Testimonials.q2: The pile is so dense your foot sinks a quarter inch. I notice it every morning.
Testimonials.q3: Specified for a client in Geneva. They wrote a year later just to say the rug had aged better than the room around it.

Doors.leftBody: Tell us about the room, the light, the way the day moves through it. We'll suggest a piece, or commission a new one.
Doors.rightBody: Trade pricing, full collection access, project samples, and a dedicated point of contact for specification.

Archive.title: The Archive
Archive.subtitle: All 24 pieces. Each woven by one artisan over six to twelve months.

Trade.title: For Interior Designers
Trade.intro1: We work directly with architects, interior designers, and hospitality professionals to source, customize, and commission handmade rugs for specific projects.
Trade.intro2: Our trade program is designed to simplify the specification process, providing you with dedicated support from initial concept to final installation.
Trade.benefitsLead: As a registered trade partner, you receive:
Trade.benefit1Body: Exclusive discounts across our entire collection.
Trade.benefit2Body: Alter the size, scale, or color palette of any existing design to fit your project.
Trade.benefit3Body: Work with our design team in Jaipur to weave a completely new, one-of-one piece.
Trade.benefit4Body: Complimentary strike-offs and material samples shipped directly to your studio.
Trade.benefit5Body: A single point of contact to manage timelines, freight, and installation logistics.
Trade.step1Body: Share your floor plans, fabric swatches, or mood boards. We'll recommend pieces from the archive or begin drafting a custom design.
Trade.step2Body: For custom orders, we dye the yarn and weave a small 2x2ft sample (a strike-off) for your approval before putting the full piece on the loom.
Trade.step3Body: Once approved, the rug is woven. Depending on the knot count and scale, this takes between 12 to 36 weeks. We provide regular visual updates from the loom.
Trade.quote: Carpetstory understood exactly what the space needed. The custom piece they wove for the London townhouse is the anchor of the entire room.
Trade.openAccountBody: To apply for a trade account, please email your business details, resale certificate, and website to our trade team.

TwoDoors.tradeBody: Access our trade portal for tear sheets, trade pricing, and custom commissions.
TwoDoors.commissionBody: Speak directly with our atelier about sourcing a piece from the archive or commissioning a custom size.

TradePage.title: Partner With Us
TradePage.intro: A great carpet is not made quickly. Neither is a great manufacturing house. Carpetstory produces the full spectrum of carpets and rugs from Jaipur — supplying trade professionals and luxury brands across more than thirty countries with rigour and discretion. We manufacture everything. We compromise on nothing.
TradePage.pillar1Body: From hand-knotted artisan rugs to precision machine-made carpets, we produce every construction and every specification — for designers, importers, luxury brands, and retailers. Whatever your floor demands, we make it.
TradePage.pillar2Body: Built with patience, to supply the world’s finest interiors. Full export infrastructure, milestone transparency, and the kind of discretion that established luxury houses quietly depend on.
TradePage.pillar3Body: A full-spectrum carpet manufacturing and export house in Jaipur. We partner with those who demand a manufacturer they never have to question. If that is you, you are in the right place.
TradePage.partner1Body: We manufacture to your exact project specification, size, construction, material, and palette, with the precision and lead-time discipline that professional projects demand.
TradePage.partner2Body: Our production infrastructure supports high-volume, repeat-order supply with full export documentation, private label manufacturing, and consistent quality across every shipment.
TradePage.partner3Body: We supply curated, commercially ready collections to retail and showroom partners with the exclusivity, reorder reliability, and brand support that serious retail operations require.
TradePage.partner4Body: We provide white label manufacturing for established luxury brands requiring a high-calibre Jaipur production partner. Your designs and standards, executed with complete discretion.
TradePage.step1Desc: Specifications, quantities, reference materials, or a product requirement.
TradePage.step2Desc: Production options, pricing, and lead times within one business day.
TradePage.step3Desc: A physical sample dispatched for review and sign-off before production.
TradePage.step4Desc: Full transparency on timeline with milestone updates and inspection.
TradePage.step5Desc: Door-to-door shipping with complete documentation and compliance.
TradePage.successMessage: Thank you for starting a B2B inquiry. Our trade team in Jaipur will review your specifications and contact you within one business day.
TradePage.sampleKitDesc: Before you specify, before you order, before you commit — hold the material, feel the construction, understand the difference. Our consultant curates the right swatches personally, based on your requirement. No image does it justice.
TradePage.sampleKitBullet1: Curated to your brief by a Carpetstory consultant
TradePage.sampleKitBullet3: 50% of the kit value redeemable on your first B2B order

CollectionsPage.headline: Twelve houses. One workshop.
CollectionsPage.description: From Persian archive patterns to Moroccan long pile, every collection is woven in Jaipur to the same standard of construction.

RugsPage.title: Every piece in the workshop.
RugsPage.subtitle: {total} rugs across {count} collections. Filter, shortlist, and share.
RugsPage.calcDesc: A rough range to begin the conversation — not a quote. Tell us the construction, and we'll sketch the cost.

CraftPage.title: The Craft
CraftPage.description: An uncompromising dedication to traditional hand-knotting techniques, preserving a centuries-old art form in the heart of Jaipur.

AboutPage.title: About Carpetstory
AboutPage.metaTitle: About Carpetstory — Our Heritage and Vision
AboutPage.metaDescription: Discover the story behind Carpetstory. Four generations of master weavers in Jaipur, Rajasthan. Traditional hand-knotted techniques and family vision.

ContactPage.title: Contact
ContactPage.metaDescription: Contact the Carpetstory team. Start a conversation about your space. We respond within 24 hours. Or message us on WhatsApp.

HeritagePage.title: Heritage
HeritagePage.metaDescription: Founded 1924. Four generations of master weavers in Jaipur — natural dyes, hand-spun wool, the Persian knot. The same madder red since the beginning.

InquiryPage.title: Inquiry
InquiryPage.metaDescription: Tell us about the space. We reply within 24 hours. Or message us directly on WhatsApp.

JournalPage.title: Journal
JournalPage.description: Notes from the atelier. Writing on craft, colour, materials, and the people who weave Carpetstory rugs.

WorldStage.label: On the World Stage
WorldStage.headline: Shown at {italic}, on the world’s largest stage.
WorldStage.body: In 2016 our hand-knotted rugs were selected for Domotex in Hannover — the world’s leading trade fair for carpets and floor coverings. Among thousands of makers from across the globe, our pieces were chosen to be shown.
